approach: Continuation of the operator kickback of split-worktree-cli-api-surfaces (that shard, seq 5): complete the CLI-surface separation across the whole CLI. One cli/X mirror node per library node holds that capability's CLI presentation & invocation; the surface-independent contract stays in the capability node. The narrow divergence-only framing is revised to a consistent CLI-surface axis (root spec.md placement map updated). Classification comes from the full exploration map (command->node, per-scenario S/C/M).

Blast radius LARGE but spec-mostly: new nodes cli/detection, cli/placement, cli/lookup, cli/driving, cli/template/{apply,capture}, each trimming its frozen library counterpart (mux/detection, mux/placement, mux/lookup, mux/driving, template/apply, template/capture). Re-opens six frozen suites — each a relocation-with-conservation (S scenarios move to cli/X, C stay), so no acceptance is weakened. The read/close/focus verbs are currently UNSPECIFIED and get filled in as backfill to observable behavior (the verbs already exist in cli.ts); if that shows a behavior gap needing source, it becomes its own build-to-contract step. Package 0.0.0 (Compatibility inert).

Leash auto-spec: high novelty (generalizes the surface axis corpus-wide) but each per-node split is the same relocation-with-conservation shape the worktree split already proved; coverage conservation is checked per node by the cold judge. Self-assert within the spec leash and land provisionally for operator ratification of the whole reorg.

why.floor: Clearance fires and CLEARS across six frozen library suites (detection, driving, lookup, placement, template/apply, template/capture), each re-opened by a relocation-with-conservation: the CLI-surface scenarios move to a new cli/X mirror node, the contract stays. Ratified by the operator kickback mandate (complete the CLI surface). Four independent cold spec-judges verified coverage conservation scenario-by-scenario against the HEAD baseline — none lost, none narrowed. cli/lookup adds 4 NEW frozen scenarios for the previously-unspecified read/focus/close verbs, backfilled to observable behavior and confirmed accurate to src/cli.ts by the lookup judge. No Conflict. Compatibility inert (package 0.0.0).
why.blast: large but spec-mostly — 6 new cli/X mirror nodes (+ cli/template index), 6 trimmed library suites, cli/README.md + root placement-map reframed to the CLI-surface axis. The only source touched is 3 added verb-action tests (no product code changed; read/focus/close already shipped).
why.novelty: high — generalizes the surface axis corpus-wide (worktree was the first instance); establishes cli/X-mirrors-mux/X as the standing pattern.
why.confidence: high — 4 cold spec-judges: detection+driving ALIGNED (byte-identical moves), lookup ALIGNED (coverage conserved + 4 backfilled verbs accurate to source), template ALIGNED (apply 69->69, capture 33->35, mixed calls judged correct), placement returned architect FAIL on ONE misfiled scenario (--at-omitted fallback pinned a CLI default the adapter actually makes) — remediated by relocating it to mux/placement re-altituded to the adapter contract, which otherwise had no coverage of its own fallback. Post-remediation all suites 1:1 (14 features parse), check-spec-state OK. Self-asserted within the auto-spec leash; lands provisionally for operator ratification of the whole reorg.

why.floor: none. The reorg is spec relocation of already-verified behavior EXCEPT the 4 new read/focus/close frozen scenarios. close was already verified (resolution tests assert kill-pane); read (raw bytes to stdout, not the JSON envelope), read --lines (capture-pane -S -n), and focus (switch-client -> select-window -> select-pane, empty stdout) had no direct happy-path verification, so 3 tests were added. Every other relocated scenario keeps its pre-existing passing verification.
why.blast: small — 3 added verification tests; no product-code change (read/focus/close already shipped).
why.novelty: low-moderate — verifications for verbs that shipped without spec coverage.
why.confidence: high — the 3 new tests pass and each falsifies a wrong subject (JSON-wrapped read, ignored --lines, no-op focus); the lookup cold spec-judge independently confirmed the 4 scenarios match src/cli.ts (readCommand/focusCommand/closeCommand) and the tmux/herdr adapters. Full suite to be confirmed green by pnpm verify at handoff. Self-asserted within the auto-spec leash.

note: mux/README.md (the mux/ descriptive index) behavior-map rollup still attributes CLI read-outs/usage-errors (doctor hint, mode, bare-send exit-2, etc.) to the mux units in its detailed 'every scenario maps to' table. A note above the unit table now points the CLI surface to cli/X, but the row-level prose sync remains. Descriptive index only (ungated, no suite), so non-blocking; sync it when next touching mux/README.md.

Heading convention: every mux/*/README.md (and the new cli/* nodes mirroring them) uses '## Logic' where sdd:spec-format-governance names the section '## Control Flow'. Uniform corpus-wide convention (pre-existing at HEAD, not introduced here). Either a project-root governance override declaring the local heading, or a corpus-wide rename, would stop it reading as drift at every future gate. Flagged by the placement judge.

Minor prose/consistency nits from the cold judges, all non-blocking: (a) mux/detection Use Cases bullet names only tmux/herdr though its Outline covers wezterm/zellij (pre-existing); (b) template/capture engine has three sibling adapter-refusal scenarios now using two Then-vocabularies (two got CLI mirrors, the algorithm-invariant no-valid-split-sequence one did not) — record the rule so the next CR inherits it; (c) cli/lookup and mux/lookup .feature section order does not match their README Use Cases order (presentational); (d) a small candidate-fields duplication surface between mux/lookup ambiguity and cli/lookup ambiguity-report to watch.
